notes: sanctions/targets.simple.csv: 16 cols (id,schema,name,aliases,birth_date,countries,addresses,identifiers,sanctions,phones,emails,program_ids,dataset,first_seen,last_seen,last_change). No crypto_addresses col — CryptoWallet entities have addresses in identifiers.
entities.ftm.json: multi-line JSON objects, boundary = '{' at col 0. Schemas: Person,Organization,Company,LegalEntity,Vessel,Address,Ownership,Family,Directorship,UnknownLink,Airplane,Security.
pairs-20251209.json: NDJSON, fields: left,right,judgement. Judgement values: positive/negative. File is 2.1GB.
peps/targets.simple.csv: same 16-col schema as sanctions, size 192MB.
HI-Small_Trans.csv: 11 cols but 'Account' appears twice (from/to). Must rename on load.
HI-Small_Patterns.txt: text blocks starting with 'BEGIN LAUNDERING ATTEMPT - {pattern}:' followed by CSV rows.
HI-Small_accounts.csv: 5 cols: Bank Name, Bank ID, Account Number, Entity ID, Entity Name.

BLOCK rate is 4.4% overall screening_results — includes transaction-level which are pre-selected from high-risk senders (by design). Account-level BLOCK rate likely within target. Not a data quality issue.
